# mcp-proxy-plus

> Production-grade MCP gateway. Manages multiple MCP servers behind a single HTTP endpoint with lazy reconnect, health monitoring, and API key auth.

## Overview

mcp-proxy-plus is a reverse proxy that aggregates multiple MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers into one Streamable HTTP endpoint. Clients connect to a single port and access all configured servers via path-based routing.

## Key Features

- Single endpoint: all MCP servers accessible via `/servers/{name}/mcp`
- Lazy reconnect (v1.1.0): crashed servers auto-respawn on next request
- Health monitoring: `/health` and `/status` endpoints
- API key authentication: Bearer token middleware
- Stdio/SSE/Streamable HTTP transport support
- JSON config: declarative server definitions with env vars and args

## Architecture

```
Client (Kiro CLI) → :3100/servers/{name}/mcp → mcp-proxy → spawn/connect → MCP Server
```

## Configuration

`mcp-proxy-config.json`:
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "memory-service": {
      "command": "uv", "args": ["run", "memory-server"],
      "env": {"MCP_MEMORY_DIR": "/path/to/db"}
    }
  }
}
```

## Installation

```bash
uv tool install --from git+https://github.com/filhocf/mcp-proxy mcp-proxy-plus
```

## Links

- GitHub: https://github.com/filhocf/mcp-proxy
